title: noronhia obtusifolia
intro: Noronhia obtusifolia, also known as the Madagascar ironwood or the "iron tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies for its perceived health benefits.
